Maoist regime in China is responsible for a large number of deaths during its rule, particularly during the Great Leap Forward campaign (1958-1962) and the Cultural Revolution (1966-1976).

This led to the persecution, torture, and execution of millions of people, including intellectuals, artists, and other "bourgeois" elements. Overall, Maoist policies and actions resulted in the deaths of an estimated 40 to 70 million people in China.
